plan.failureSummary: The test assertion for the final URL is too strict. It expects 'https://www.checklyhq.com/docs/constructs/playwright-check/' but received 'https://www.checklyhq.com/docs/constructs/playwright-check/#playwright-check-suite'. The autonomous agent correctly selected the 'Constructs > DETECT' search result, which naturally deep-links to the section hash.
observedIndicators: Assertion failure: URL mismatch (exact string vs hash fragment)
AI succeeded in completing the search objective
Tool call parameters show the selected link explicitly contains the hash: #playwright-check-suite
History shows a sudden regression from success to a 4-run failure streak
Baseline and failure screenshots show the same visual state, confirming the navigation target is correct

remediationSteps.summary: Loosen the URL assertion to accommodate deep-link hash fragments.
remediationSteps.details: Change the assertion in 'tests/checkly.test.ts' from .toEqual() to .toContain() or use a regex to ignore the trailing hash fragment. For example: expect(page.url()).toContain('/docs/constructs/playwright-check/')
Once the code is updated, run the test again to confirm it passes with the relaxed assertion.

plan.notes: The AI performed the search and selection correctly. The failure is purely due to a string mismatch in the deterministic Playwright assertion. The 4-run failure streak indicates this is a persistent change in the website's search result links or deep-linking behavior.
